evals.prompt: What is the CPU usage right now on all hosts?
evals.expected_output: Returns live CPU percentages per host from Prometheus, includes Grafana Explore link
expectations: Response includes CPU percentage values per host/instance
Data source is identified as Prometheus (live)
A Grafana Explore link or deep-link is included
The query was routed through prometheus_live path

evals.prompt: Show me all error logs from the last hour
evals.expected_output: Returns ERROR/FATAL log entries from Elasticsearch with service names and timestamps
expectations: Response includes log entries with ERROR or FATAL level
Each log entry has a timestamp and service/host source
Data was retrieved from Elasticsearch (infra-logs index)
The query was routed through log_search path

evals.expected_output: Returns active Prometheus alerts with severity, instance, and description
expectations: Response lists firing alerts with alertname and severity
Each alert includes the affected instance
Data source is Prometheus alerts API (not ES historical)
The query was routed through prometheus_live or alert_search path

evals.prompt: Show me the Grafana dashboard for web-01
evals.expected_output: Returns Grafana dashboard links with var-host=web-01 substitution
expectations: Response includes clickable Grafana dashboard URLs
Dashboard URLs include var-host=web-01 parameter
Panel titles and types are listed for relevant dashboards
The query was routed through grafana_dashboard path

evals.prompt: Why is the API server down?
evals.expected_output: Cross-domain investigation combining Prometheus up status, ES error logs, and ES alerts
expectations: Response combines data from multiple sources (Prometheus + ES)
Live metric status (up/down) is included
Recent error logs related to the API server are shown
Recent alerts for the API server are shown if any
Data source is identified as multi (prometheus + elasticsearch)
